_comment: Curated per-animation model-part swaps for moves whose part change is driven by hardcoded special-move C code (NOT a subaction code-31 'Change Model State' event), so the auto path in StreamingServer can't see them. Keyed by the fighter-data ftData root name. Each entry: 'match' = substring of the animation symbol, 'slot'/'variant' = the HighPoly slot and option to select (variant 1 = the 'ball' form). Source: melee decomp ftParts_80074B0C calls. See docs/ANIMATION_PART_VISIBILITY.md.

ftDataKoopa.note: Whirling Fortress (up-B) -> shell, limbs tucked. The withdraw fn ftKp_SpecialN_80135780 (named for its address-range file SpecialN.c) is CALLED by ftKp_SpecialHi.c -> the swap belongs to up-B, not neutral-B (neutral-B = Fire Breath, no shell). Verified by decomp call-graph + render.

ftDataGkoopa.note: Giga up-B withdraws too: ftGk_Init.c registers Bowser's ftKp_SpecialHi_* handlers, which call the same ftKp_SpecialN_80135780 shell withdraw. (Per-dir decomp scan misses this cross-char handler reuse; confirmed by ftGk_Init.c + in-game.) slot 0 var 1 = Giga shell, same as its code-31 aerials.
